---
title: "denet profiling overhead and detail in a snakemake alignment workflow"
output:
  html_document:
    fig_caption: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,
                      fig.width = 10, fig.height = 8)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(jsonlite)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
library(stringr)
```

```{r load-benchmark-tsv}
step_labels <- c(
  simulate_genome = "Genome\nsimulation",
  index_genome    = "Genome\nindexing",
  simulate_reads  = "Read\nsimulation",
  align_sort_bam  = "Align, sort\n& index"
)

steps <- names(step_labels)

read_benchmarks <- function(outdir, condition) {
  lapply(steps, function(step) {
    path <- file.path(outdir, "benchmarks", paste0(step, ".tsv"))
    df <- read.table(path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t")
    df$step <- step
    df$condition <- condition
    df$repeat_id <- seq_len(nrow(df))
    df
  }) |> bind_rows()
}

baseline <- read_benchmarks("results_baseline", "Snakemake only")
denet    <- read_benchmarks("results_denet",    "Snakemake + denet")

bench <- bind_rows(baseline, denet) |>
  mutate(
    step_label = factor(step_labels[step], levels = step_labels),
    condition  = factor(condition, levels = c("Snakemake only", "Snakemake + denet"))
  )
```

```{r load-denet-jsonl}
parse_denet_jsonl <- function(path) {
  lines <- readLines(path)
  # first line is metadata (has "pid" key), remaining lines are samples
  samples <- lines[sapply(lines, function(l) grepl('"ts_ms"', l))]
  parsed <- lapply(samples, function(l) {
    obj <- fromJSON(l)
    agg <- obj$aggregated
    data.frame(
      ts_ms         = agg$ts_ms,
      cpu_usage     = agg$cpu_usage,
      mem_rss_mb    = agg$mem_rss_kb / 1024,
      disk_read_mb  = agg$disk_read_bytes / 1024^2,
      disk_write_mb = agg$disk_write_bytes / 1024^2,
      thread_count  = agg$thread_count
    )
  })
  df <- bind_rows(parsed)
  # relative time from start of first sample
  df$time_s <- (df$ts_ms - df$ts_ms[1]) / 1000
  df
}

denet_metrics_dir <- "results_denet/denet_metrics"

denet_files <- list.files(denet_metrics_dir, pattern = "\\.jsonl$", full.names = TRUE)

denet_ts <- lapply(denet_files, function(f) {
  step <- str_remove(basename(f), "\\.jsonl$")
  # each repeat appends to the same file; split on metadata lines
  lines <- readLines(f)
  meta_idx <- which(grepl('"pid"', lines))
  run_list <- lapply(seq_along(meta_idx), function(i) {
    start <- meta_idx[i] + 1
    end   <- if (i < length(meta_idx)) meta_idx[i + 1] - 1 else length(lines)
    block <- lines[start:end]
    block <- block[nzchar(block) & grepl('"ts_ms"', block)]
    if (length(block) == 0) return(NULL)
    parsed <- lapply(block, function(l) {
      obj <- fromJSON(l)
      agg <- obj$aggregated
      data.frame(
        ts_ms         = agg$ts_ms,
        cpu_usage     = agg$cpu_usage,
        mem_rss_mb    = agg$mem_rss_kb / 1024,
        disk_write_mb = agg$disk_write_bytes / 1024^2
      )
    })
    df <- bind_rows(parsed)
    df$time_s  <- (df$ts_ms - df$ts_ms[1]) / 1000
    df$repeat_id <- i
    df
  })
  df <- bind_rows(run_list)
  df$step <- step
  df
}) |> bind_rows() |>
  mutate(step_label = factor(step_labels[step], levels = step_labels))
```

```{r fig-wall-time}
# n_repeats is read from data so the subtitle stays accurate if benchmark_repeats changes
n_repeats <- bench |>
  distinct(step_label, condition, repeat_id) |>
  count(step_label, condition) |>
  pull(n) |>
  max()

bench_summary <- bench |>
  group_by(step_label, condition) |>
  summarise(
    mean_s = mean(s),
    sd_s   = sd(s),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Error bars show +/- 1 SD across repeats.
# SD (rather than SE or CV) is used because we want to convey the absolute
# run-to-run spread in seconds, not the precision of the mean estimate.
p_time <- ggplot(bench_summary,
       aes(x = step_label, y = mean_s, fill = condition,
           ymin = mean_s - sd_s, ymax = mean_s + sd_s)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.75), width = 0.65) +
  geom_errorbar(position = position_dodge(0.75), width = 0.25) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Snakemake only" = "#4C72B0",
                               "Snakemake + denet" = "#DD8452")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Wall-clock time (s)", fill = NULL,
       title = "A  Wall-clock time per step",
       subtitle = paste0("mean +/- 1 SD, n = ", n_repeats, " repeats")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())
```

```{r fig-max-rss}
rss_summary <- bench |>
  group_by(step_label, condition) |>
  summarise(
    mean_rss = mean(max_rss),
    sd_rss   = sd(max_rss),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )

p_rss <- ggplot(rss_summary,
       aes(x = step_label, y = mean_rss, fill = condition,
           ymin = mean_rss - sd_rss, ymax = mean_rss + sd_rss)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.75), width = 0.65) +
  geom_errorbar(position = position_dodge(0.75), width = 0.25) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Snakemake only" = "#4C72B0",
                               "Snakemake + denet" = "#DD8452")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Peak RSS (MB)", fill = NULL,
       title = "B  Peak resident memory per step",
       subtitle = paste0("mean +/- 1 SD, n = ", n_repeats, " repeats")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())
```

```{r fig-cpu-timeseries}
# All rules shown, each repeat as its own line, colored by step.
# time_s is relative to the start of each repeat so rules with different
# durations are comparable on the same x-axis.
p_cpu <- ggplot(denet_ts,
                aes(x = time_s, y = cpu_usage,
                    color = step_label,
                    group = interaction(step_label, repeat_id))) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(x = "Elapsed time (s)", y = "CPU usage (%)", color = NULL,
       title = "C  CPU usage per rule (all repeats)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r fig-mem-timeseries}
p_mem <- ggplot(denet_ts,
                aes(x = time_s, y = mem_rss_mb,
                    color = step_label,
                    group = interaction(step_label, repeat_id))) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(x = "Elapsed time (s)", y = "RSS (MB)", color = NULL,
       title = "D  Memory usage per rule (all repeats)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r combined-figure, fig.cap="Resource profiling of a simulated read alignment workflow. (A, B) Mean wall-clock time and peak RSS per pipeline step; error bars are +/- 1 SD across benchmark repeats. SD was chosen over SE or CV to express absolute run-to-run variability in interpretable units. (C, D) CPU and RSS timeseries recorded by denet at 100 ms intervals for all four rules; each line is one repeat, colored by rule. Elapsed time is relative to the start of each repeat."}
(p_time | p_rss) / (p_cpu | p_mem) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```
